raw:
{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.3", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.3": 5,
    "R1.4": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 4,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The revised text presents a clear and concise conceptual reconstruction of the AskFirst policy, identifying the load-bearing concept (abstention head) and proposing a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.3": "The revised text provides evidence-claim alignment by auditing every load-bearing claim against the corresponding table/figure, specifically the evaluation results on the held-out test set.",
    "R1.4": "The revised text presents a novelty assessment by mapping against the nearest 3-5 works and identifying sharp vs rhetorical delta, although it is limited to synthetic data and does not generalize to real-world manipulation.",
    "RX.1": "The revised text discloses uncertainty for each recommendation, marking confidence and missing evidence in the evaluation results.",
    "RX.3": "The revised text adheres to the scope by covering exactly the agreed J*(d), although it is limited to synthetic data and does not generalize to real-world manipulation.",
    "RX.4": "The revised text avoids downstream harm by being harm-free, but with one minor regression in the deployment claim (8 Hz figure may be simulated).",
    "RX.5": "The revised text provides provenance and traceability by releasing the synthetic corpus, generation script, abstention-head checkpoints, and calibration split to support reproduction."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.3": 0.9,
    "R1.4": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The revised text presents a clear and concise conceptual reconstruction of the AskFirst policy, identifying the load-bearing concept (abstention head) and proposing a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.3: The revised text provides evidence-claim alignment by auditing every load-bearing claim against the corresponding table/figure, specifically the evaluation results on the held-out test set.
rationale.R1.4: The revised text presents a novelty assessment by mapping against the nearest 3-5 works and identifying sharp vs rhetorical delta, although it is limited to synthetic data and does not generalize to real-world manipulation.
rationale.RX.1: The revised text discloses uncertainty for each recommendation, marking confidence and missing evidence in the evaluation results.
rationale.RX.3: The revised text adheres to the scope by covering exactly the agreed J*(d), although it is limited to synthetic data and does not generalize to real-world manipulation.
rationale.RX.4: The revised text avoids downstream harm by being harm-free, but with one minor regression in the deployment claim (8 Hz figure may be simulated).
rationale.RX.5: The revised text provides provenance and traceability by releasing the synthetic corpus, generation script, abstention-head checkpoints, and calibration split to support reproduction.
